QF.id: Issue Management - General Obligations and Due Diligence_11

question.text: For a RIGHTS ISSUE, the initial post-issue report must be submitted within ______ of issue closure, and the final report within ______ of finalization of basis of allotment.

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Rights issues have a two-stage post-issue reporting requirement: (1) Initial report (Part B of Schedule XVII) within 3 days of issue closure, and (2) Final report (Part C of Schedule XVII) within 15 days of finalization of basis of allotment. The due diligence certificate (Form F of Schedule V) accompanies the final report.
**The Trap:** "7 days; 7 days" is the timeline for public issues (only one report, within 7 days). Candidates often mix up the public issue and rights issue timelines. Remember: Rights = 3+15, Public = 7.
**Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX exam frequently tests timeline-based questions. The 3-day and 15-day deadlines for rights issues are among the most commonly tested regulatory timelines.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Mnemonic: "R-3-15" — Rights issue, 3 days initial, 15 days final. "P-7" — Public issue, 7 days final. Write this on your rough sheet at the start of the exam.
